additional_metadata.note: First official dbt Meetup in Spain, hosted online.

events.agenda: Inaugural Barcelona dbt Meetup with a Q&A with dbt co-founder Drew Banin and two case-study talks on adopting dbt and marketing pipelines with AWS.
events.event_description: First official dbt Meetup in Spain, an online networking event for the data community.
events.raw_text:
Wed, Oct 27, 2021 · 6:30 PM to 8:00 PM CEST

Online event

Barcelona dbt Meetup (Online)

Agenda:
6:35 Welcome Remarks by Guillaume Legoy
6:50 Q&A with Drew Banin
7:05 Adopting dbt in Lifull Connect by Adela Tort & David Martinez
7:25 Marketing Pipeline using AWS & dbt by Frederick Ayala Gomez
7:45 Closing Remarks

events.agenda: First 2023 Barcelona dbt Meetup covering dbt for data science prediction storage at Codat and a large-scale migration to dbt at Gameloft.
events.event_description: dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data, with talks from community members on analytics engineering, data stacks, modeling, and testing.
events.location: Spaces 22 Arroba Mar, Carrer de la Marina, 16-18, Barcelona
events.raw_text:
Thu, Feb 9, 2023 · 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM CET

Spaces 22 Arroba Mar, Barcelona

Barcelona dbt Meetup (in-person) 2023

Talk #1: Hugo, Head Data Engineering at Codat - Using dbt to store predictions and view accuracy metrics for data science over time, covering workflow orchestration and dbt testing to hone model accuracy.

Talk #2: Adriana & Daniel, Data Engineering Team at Gameloft - Presenting the data stack at Gameloft and their migration to dbt of more than 1000 different models with complex transformations, custom materializations, and a dbt-metadata-based alert system.

talks.description: Using dbt to store data science predictions and track model accuracy metrics over time, including workflow orchestration and testing.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 🗣 Hugo, Head Data Engineering at Codat - Using dbt to store predictions and view accuracy metrics for data science over time.
talks.title: Using dbt to store predictions and view accuracy metrics for data science over time

talks.description: Gameloft's migration of over 1000 complex models to dbt, covering custom materializations, a metadata-based alert system, and improved data exploration.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 🗣 Adriana & Daniel, Data Engineering Team at Gameloft - Presenting the data stack at Gameloft and sharing their experience while preparing a migration to dbt of more than 1000 different models
talks.title: Migrating 1000+ models to dbt at Gameloft

events.agenda: Talks on Landbot's data pipeline monitoring with Fivetran/dbt and Heygo's migration from BI-managed aggregations to a Star schema in dbt.
events.event_description: dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data, with talks predominantly focused on community members' experience with dbt.
events.location: Ironhack Barcelona Tech bootcamps, C/ de Pamplona, 96, Barcelona
events.raw_text:
Thu, Oct 13, 2022 · 6:30 PM to 9:00 PM CEST

Ironhack Barcelona Tech bootcamps, Barcelona

Barcelona dbt Meetup (in-person)

Talk #1: Patricio Villanueva, Data Analyst at Landbot - Landbot's data pipeline from fivetran, passing from dbt and how they monitor errors and failure rates. Covers ingestion (airflow, fivetran, stitch), dbt experience (test, CI, macros) and pipeline monitoring.

Talk #2: Eduardo Varas, Data Analytics Engineer at Spaulding Ridge - From BI managed aggregations to Star data model in dbt. Heygo's dbt project migrating from a complex BI aggregation model to a more robust Star model, on Fivetran, Snowflake and Looker.

talks.description: Landbot's data ingestion pipeline (Fivetran, Airflow, Stitch) into dbt, and how the team monitors pipeline errors and failure rates using dbt tests, CI, and macros.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 🗣 Patricio Villanueva, Data Analyst at Landbot - Landbot's data pipeline from fivetran, passing from dbt and how we monitor errors and our failures rates
talks.title: Landbot's data pipeline from Fivetran, dbt, and monitoring failure rates

talks.description: Heygo's migration from complex BI-managed aggregations to a robust Star schema data model built in dbt on Fivetran, Snowflake, and Looker.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 🗣 Eduardo Varas, Data Analytics Engineer at Spaulding Ridge - From BI managed aggregations to Star data model in dbt
talks.title: From BI managed aggregations to Star data model in dbt

events.agenda: In-person meetup covering carbon analytics with dbt and scaling a dbt project at Wallbox.
events.event_description: In-person dbt meetup for the Barcelona data community, following a period without in-person events.
events.location: Scopely - Sant Gervasi, Carrer de Folgueroles 17-25, Barcelona
events.raw_text:
Tue, Jul 5, 2022 · 6:30 PM to 9:00 PM CEST

Scopely - Sant Gervasi, Barcelona

Barcelona dbt Meetup (in-person)

Talk #1: Nina Anderson, Technical Lead at Infinite Lambda - Carbon analytics with dbt. Argues carbon analytics should be given the same status as traditional BI, presenting progress on an open source tool at Infinite Lambda to help companies estimate carbon footprint using dbt and Snowflake.

Talk #2: Anna Julià, Product Data Analyst & Miguel Llorente, Senior Analytics Engineer at Wallbox - How to scale a dbt project at a scale level. Wallbox's data team scaled to 20 people and hundreds of production models; challenges include dbt core and marts, dbt orchestration with airflow, dbt alerting and monitoring, CI/CD.

talks.description: Argument for treating carbon analytics with the same rigor as traditional BI, and progress on an open-source dbt+Snowflake tool for estimating carbon footprint.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 🗣 Nina Anderson, Technical Lead at Infinite Lambda - Carbon analytics with dbt
talks.title: Carbon analytics with dbt

talks.description: How Wallbox scaled their dbt project to hundreds of production models across a 20-person data team, covering orchestration, alerting, monitoring, and CI/CD.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 🗣 Anna Julià, Product Data Analyst & Miguel Llorente, Senior Analytics Engineer at Wallbox - How to scale a dbt project at a scale level
talks.speaker_title: Product Data Analyst & Senior Analytics Engineer, Wallbox
talks.title: How to scale a dbt project at a scale level

events.agenda: Talks on Carta's self-service BI to CI/testing dbt journey and using dbt with open source tools for health data modeling at Barcelona Supercomputing Center.
events.event_description: dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data, with talks from community members on analytics engineering, data stacks, modeling, and testing.
events.location: Spaces 22 Arroba, C. de Pallars, 193, Barcelona
events.raw_text:
Thu, Oct 26, 2023 · 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM CEST

Spaces 22 Arroba, Barcelona

Barcelona dbt Meetup (in-person) October 2023

Talk #1: From Self-service BI to CI and testing: The dbt journey at Carta - Aleix Cubel Capdevila. Carta Europe's self-serve analytics approach, stack and processes, and deep dive into testing and CI setup.

Talk #2: Leveraging an Open Source Stack: Modelling Health Data at Barcelona Supercomputing Center - Alberto Labarga. Using dbt for standardization of clinical data from different hospitals into the OMOP-CDM data model, alongside meltano, lightdash, and openmetadata.

talks.description: Carta Europe's journey building a self-service BI approach, then adding CI and testing rigor to their dbt setup.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: From Self-service BI to CI and testing: The dbt journey at Carta - Aleix Cubel Capdevila
talks.title: From Self-service BI to CI and testing: The dbt journey at Carta

talks.description: Using dbt and an open-source stack (meltano, lightdash, openmetadata) to standardize clinical data from multiple hospitals into the OMOP-CDM data model.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Leveraging an Open Source Stack: Modelling Health Data at Barcelona Supercomputing Center - Alberto Labarga
talks.title: Leveraging an Open Source Stack: Modelling Health Data at Barcelona Supercomputing Center

events.agenda: Talks on cost-effective marketing analytics with dbt, Athena and AWS at Bandai Namco Mobile, unlocking self-service with the dbt Semantic Layer, and a polyglot data pipeline with Google Cloud.
events.event_description: dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data, with talks from community members on analytics engineering, data stacks, modeling, and testing.
events.location: Spaces - Barcelona, Plaza Universitat, Gran Via de les Corts Catalanes, 583, Barcelona
events.raw_text:
Thu, Jun 22, 2023 · 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM CEST

Spaces Universitat, Barcelona

Barcelona dbt Meetup (in-person) June 2023

Talk #1: The Goblin's Guide to dbt: Pipelines using Athena and AWS - Frederick Ayala. How Bandai Namco Mobile's marketing team uses dbt, Athena, and AWS to monitor their marketing pipeline, from data collection to ML models and dbt runs.

Talk #2: Bringing self-service to the next level with the dbt Semantic Layer - Sergi Gómez. Leveraging the dbt Semantic Layer to eliminate bottlenecks and give data teams and domain experts more autonomy, with governance lessons from Saivo.

Talk #3: Building a polyglot data pipeline with Google Cloud - Luis Velasco. Combining dbt python models, Spark serverless, and traditional dbt with BigQuery.

talks.description: How Bandai Namco Mobile's marketing team uses dbt, Athena, and AWS services to build a cost-effective marketing analytics pipeline, from data collection through ML models.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: The Goblin's Guide to dbt: Pipelines using Athena and AWS - by Frederick Ayala
talks.title: The Goblin's Guide to dbt: Pipelines using Athena and AWS

talks.description: Using the dbt Semantic Layer to remove bottlenecks and give data teams and domain experts more autonomy in defining semantic components, with governance practices from Saivo.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Bringing self-service to the next level with the dbt Semantic Layer - by Sergi Gómez
talks.title: Bringing self-service to the next level with the dbt Semantic Layer

talks.description: Building a polyglot data pipeline combining dbt Python models, Spark serverless, and traditional dbt with BigQuery.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Building a polyglot data pipeline with Google Cloud - by Luis Velasco
talks.title: Building a polyglot data pipeline with Google Cloud

events.agenda: First 2024 Barcelona dbt Meetup covering dbt data alerting best practices and optimizing dbt model build costs using window functions.
events.event_description: dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data, with talks from community members on analytics engineering, data stacks, modeling, and testing.
events.location: Spaces - Barcelona, Plaza Universitat, Gran Via de les Corts Catalanes, 583, Barcelona
events.raw_text:
Thu, Feb 1, 2024 · 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM CET

Spaces - Barcelona, Plaza Universitat, Barcelona

Barcelona dbt Meetup (in-person) February 2024

Talk #1: Data Alerts are Hard - Mikkel Dengsøe, Co-founder @ Synq. Tips and tricks for building a robust alerting system in dbt: surfacing metadata, getting alerts to stakeholders outside the data team, integrating alerts into workflows.

Talk #2: Bringing dbt model costs to a Fraction (and when you really need window functions) - Daniel Quer Castells, Analytics Engineer @ Tier Mobility. How Tier's team optimized the build of their dbt models, starting with window functions.

talks.description: Tips and tricks for building a robust dbt-based data alerting system, covering metadata surfacing, stakeholder communication, and workflow integration.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Data Alerts are Hard - Mikkel Dengsøe, Co-founder @ Synq
talks.title: Data Alerts are Hard

talks.description: How Tier Mobility optimized dbt model build costs, starting with strategic use of window functions.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Bringing dbt model costs to a Fraction (and when you really need window functions) - Daniel Quer Castells, Analytics Engineer @ Tier Mobility
talks.title: Bringing dbt model costs to a Fraction (and when you really need window functions)

additional_metadata.note: Special edition sponsored directly by dbt Labs.

events.agenda: dbt Labs-sponsored edition featuring Tous' cloud data modernization and a talk on when to use Looker vs. dbt for business logic.
events.event_description: A special edition meetup sponsored by dbt Labs, focused on best practices applicable to both dbt Core and dbt Cloud.
events.event_name: dbt Data Stack Chat Barcelona: Tous and Euno - April 2024
events.location: Spaces - Barcelona, Spaces 22 Arroba Mar, Carrer de la Marina, 16-18, Barcelona
events.raw_text:
Thu, Apr 25, 2024 · 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM CEST

Spaces - Barcelona, Spaces 22 Arroba Mar, Barcelona

dbt Data Stack Chat Barcelona: Tous and Euno - April 2024

Talk #1: Data Modernization at international jewelry brand Tous: Migrating to cloud - Xavier Martin Ferrer, head of Data @ Tous. How dbt helped Tous transform siloed SAP and CRM data into a data model accessible across the organization.

Talk #2: Should we build this in Looker or dbt? - Sarah Levy, Co-Founder & CEO @ Euno. Proposes a 'reverse flow' approach automating integration of Looker business logic into dbt, addressing when data teams should use Looker vs dbt and governance of business logic.

talks.description: How Tous transformed siloed SAP and CRM data into an organization-wide accessible data model as part of a cloud migration using dbt.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Data Modernization at international jewelry brand Tous: Migrating to cloud - by Xavier Martin Ferrer, head of Data @ Tous
talks.title: Data Modernization at international jewelry brand Tous: Migrating to cloud

talks.description: A 'reverse flow' approach automating integration of Looker business logic into dbt, addressing when to use Looker vs. dbt and how to govern business logic across both.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Should we build this in Looker or dbt? - by Sarah Levy, Co-Founder & CEO @ Euno
talks.title: Should we build this in Looker or dbt?

Talk #1: Goodbye Legacy, Hello Lakehouse: Payfit's dbt journey - Lorena Gonzales, Analytics Engineer. Lessons learned migrating from legacy systems to a new data lakehouse using dbt, including a package for masking sensitive data early in the pipeline.

Talk #2: How a BI team adopted dbt to improve Looker performance - David Molins & Roger Torrents, BI Developer & Director Data Operations @ TravelPerk. TravelPerk's decision to move key models and business logic from their BI platform to dbt.

Talk #1: Golden Spaghetti - Afonso Moreira, Head of Data and Co-CEO @ Lovys. Refactoring a data warehouse from legacy spaghetti-like model relationships into a medallion architecture, with model labelling for improved finops.

Talk #2: Practical tips & use cases for unlocking self-service with dbt, Omni, and AI - Jon Palmer, Managing Architect @ Omni Analytics. Combining dbt, SDLC workflows, AI, and BI to improve analytical accuracy and deliver reliable, conversational insights.
